functions.author: Choascraft
functions.title: The Orca Book
pages:
The Orca Book

A Choascraft guide for orca facts, ocean keeping, riding, storage, breeding, and gear.

Fact 1

Orcas are dolphins. They are the largest members of the dolphin family.

Fact 2

Orcas live in oceans around the world, from cold polar water to warmer seas.

Fact 3

Their black and white colors help with countershading, making them harder to spot from above or below.

Fact 4

Orcas are apex predators. They can hunt fish, squid, seals, sharks, and other ocean animals.

Fact 5

Many orcas live in social pods with strong family bonds and learned hunting traditions.

Fact 6

Orcas communicate with clicks, whistles, and calls. Some groups have their own dialects.

Fact 7

Echolocation helps orcas find prey and navigate when water is dark or murky.

Fact 8

Young orcas learn from adults, including where to travel and how to hunt.

Fact 9

A tall dorsal fin helps stabilize an orca while swimming.

Fact 10

Orcas breathe air, so even ocean hunters must return to the surface.

Fact 11

Orcas can travel long distances and may change areas when food becomes scarce.

Fact 12

Healthy orca populations are tied to healthy oceans and enough prey.

Choascraft Orcas

Orcas spawn underwater in ocean biomes in small pods. They swim, flock, breach, and wander through deep water.

Wild Behavior

Wild orcas can be leashed and ridden while untamed, but they are not fully controlled until tamed.

Taming

Tame orcas with cod, salmon, or tropical fish. Tamed orcas become safer companions.

Healing

Orcas can be healed with cod, salmon, and tropical fish.

Saddles

Use a saddle on a tamed orca to make it ride-ready. Sneak or interact to manage saddle and chest actions.

Riding

A saddled tamed orca can be ridden and controlled by its owner.

Water Breathing

A saddled orca gives water breathing while mounted, making long ocean travel easier.

Storage

Use a chest on a tamed orca to give it inventory space for ocean trips.

Breeding

Tamed orcas breed with salmon. Baby orcas grow over time and follow their parents.

Babies

Baby orcas are smaller, weaker, and grow with age until they reach full size.

Drops

Orcas can drop Orca Bone, Orca Meat, Orca Tooth, Orca Blubber, Orca Shard, and the rare Orca Heart.

Zombie Orcas

Zombie orcas use the same general drop set, but Rotten Orca Blubber replaces normal blubber.

Oil Recipe

Use a brewing stand:
Input: Bucket
Reagent: Orca Blubber
Result: Bucket of Oil

Orca Core

Craft an Orca Core with:
Shard - Heart - Shard

This core is used in orca gear recipes.

Crafting Key

I = Iron Ingot
T = Orca Tooth
S = Orca Shard
C = Orca Core
B = Orca Bone
H = Orca Heart

Orca Sword

Pattern:
.T.
SIC
.B.

Result: Orca Sword

Orca Helmet

Pattern:
III
ISI
SHS

Result: Orca Helmet

Orca Chestplate

Pattern:
IHI
SCS
III

Result: Orca Chestplate

Orca Leggings

Pattern:
III
IHI
ISI

Result: Orca Leggings

Orca Boots

Pattern:
ISI
ISI
IHI

Result: Orca Boots

Netherite Orca Gear

Use a smithing table with orca gear and a netherite ingot.

Keeper Note

Feed it, saddle it, chest it, and respect the pod. The ocean is friendlier with a trained orca.
